narrative.headline: 2018 Ram 1500: electrical and engine issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Electrical system faults are the most commonly reported issue, with owners describing situations where the vehicle fails to move after shifting into drive and requires cycling through park to restore motion. Engine and transmission behavior also draws significant reports, including incidents where the truck shifts itself into park unexpectedly while moving and displays warning messages on screen. Steering lockup at road speed and fuel filler nozzle jamming at the pump round out the pattern of complaints, with several categories associated with fires or crashes.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle failing to move after being shifted into drive, with engine revving producing no forward motion until the driver cycles the shifter back through park. · 3 fire · 4 crash · 6 injured

issues.sample: I WAS DRIVING OUT OF MY DRIVE WAY, AND MY VEHICLE WENT INTO PARK BY ITSELF. IT APPEARED A STRANGE MESSAGE ON THE SCREEN SAYING 'CAR NOT IN DRIVE, SHIFT TO PARK'. ALSO MY VEHICLE DISPLAYED AN ALERT MESSAGE SAYING 'ALERT: ELECTRONIC THROTTLE CONTROL SYSTEM MALFUNCTION, THE VEHICLE
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle shifting itself into park without driver input while moving, accompanied by an on-screen warning telling the driver the car is not in drive. · 3 fire · 3 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report gas station filler nozzles locking onto the filler tube and becoming extremely difficult to remove after the pump shuts off. · 1 fire · 1 injured

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report the steering wheel locking up without warning at road speed, preventing the driver from turning the wheel to avoid obstacles. · 4 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: 58 complaints (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe the fuel pump nozzle getting stuck and being difficult to remove while refueling.
